rag_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report type mismatches or unresolved identifiers, trace the root cause to invalid helper declarations or invalid references on the same line and fix or remove the clause.

If AGREE reports 'left side of binary expression is of type int but must be of type real', ensure arithmetic expressions use consistent scalar types (int, real, bool) and avoid mixing incompatible types.
generic_rules: In AGREE contracts, always use visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not introduce new package-qualified names or types not present in the model.
For implementation-level signal routing, use assign statements only for output features or declared auxiliary variables; avoid placing guarantees in implementation scope.
When writing guarantees, ensure that all referenced identifiers are properly declared and typed, and that expressions match expected scalar types (int, real, bool).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks, and component type annexes are for assumptions/guarantees while implementation annexes are for bindings.
Use only valid AADL feature names and data types visible in the current scope; do not reference subcomponent ports or types not declared in the model.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve type errors by adding new declarations or modifying architecture; instead, fix or remove the invalid AGREE clause.
Avoid placing implementation-specific subcomponent paths or output features in component type contracts; these belong in implementation scopes.
irrelevant_topics: Component type vs implementation scope rules
